content:
🚀 poznote | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: A self-hosted platform for personal or team note-taking, documentation, and task management.
✨ Key Features: • Note-taking and documentation • Self-hosted web application • Docker support for easy installation • Git synchronization • Backup and export functionality
📖 Summary: Poznote is a self-hosted, open-source note-taking and documentation platform built with JavaScript. It allows users to create and manage notes, tasks, and wikis, with features like Git synchronization, backup/export, and offline viewing.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 colota | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling users to track their location privately and securely by sending data to their own server, with comprehensive offline capabilities.
✨ Key Features: • Self-Hosted data transmission • Privacy-focused (no analytics/telemetry) • Offline functionality (export, maps) • Encrypted backups • Location history and trip segmentation
📖 Summary: Colota is a self-hosted GPS location tracking application for Android devices. It prioritizes user privacy by sending location data directly to a user-controlled server without any analytics or telemetry, and offers robust offline functionality.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ forge | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enhancing the reliability and robustness of tool-calling and agentic workflows for self-hosted Large Language Models.
✨ Key Features: • Reliability layer for self-hosted LLM tool-calling • Guardrails: rescue parsing, retry nudges, response validation
📖 Summary: Forge is a Python framework designed to enhance the reliability of self-hosted Large Language Model (LLM) tool-calling and agentic workflows. It acts as a reliability layer, applying guardrails such as response validation, parsing rescue, and retry nudges to LLM interactions, even without predefined workflow structures. Forge can be used as a proxy server, a workflow runner, or as middleware within existing orchestration loops, supporting various local and cloud-based LLM backends.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ streambert | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified, ad-free desktop application for users to stream and download a wide variety of video content, including movies, TV series, and anime, from various online sources.
✨ Key Features: • Cross-platform Electron Desktop App • Stream Movies, TV Series, and Anime • Download Movies, TV Series, and Anime • Subtitle Management • Customizable Interface and Features
📖 Summary: Streambert is a cross-platform Electron desktop application designed for streaming and downloading movies, TV series, and anime. It offers a user-friendly interface with features like subtitle management, library tracking, and customization, while ensuring a completely ad-free and tracker-free experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
